title: Medical AI three-body diagnosis
summary: Topol's diabetic retinopathy case: AI pattern recognition, clinical expertise, and patient context must coordinate through workflow integration to produce trusted diagnosis; missing any body collapses the system into clinical uselessness.
source: Ch04 — "When Medical Intelligence Requires Three Bodies" (Topol)

nodes.label: AI Pattern Recognition
nodes.description: Retinal image classifier, 95% accurate in isolation

nodes.label: Coordination Wisdom
nodes.description: Shared decision norms, liability frame, trust

edges.description: Clinician interprets and overrides within workflow
Wisdom shapes how integration actually works

identifiability_note: Trust and norms (n6) confound both the workflow mediator and the outcome; the marginal effect of AI alone cannot be separated from the coordination culture around it.
